title: Claude Code — config snapshot tests
description:
Snapshot tests that lock down the exact Claude Code hook config BirdyBeep generates and prove patching is non-destructive against real-world `settings.json` shapes. This is the §21.1 mitigation: if Claude Code's hook format changes or our generator drifts, these tests fail loudly before anything ships.

## Scope
- Snapshot the generated BirdyBeep hook block written into `settings.json` for a from-scratch install (canonical expected JSON committed as a snapshot).
- Snapshot non-destructive patching against a set of fixture `settings.json` files representing realistic user configs: existing custom hooks (including same-event hooks), MCP server config, permissions blocks, and unusual-but-valid formatting. Assert the post-install file = the fixture + only the BirdyBeep entries, with all original content preserved.
- Include an idempotency snapshot: install twice → second output identical to first.
- Include an install→uninstall round-trip snapshot proving the file returns to the original fixture.
- Assert no token/secret/absolute-secret-path appears in any generated snapshot.
- Non-goal: live event delivery (that is CC-E2E); these are deterministic config-generation snapshots only.

## Acceptance criteria
- Committed snapshots exist for: from-scratch generation, patch-into-existing-config (multiple fixtures), double-install idempotency, and install→uninstall round-trip.
- Any change to the generated hook config or the patching behavior fails the snapshot suite until the snapshot is consciously updated.
- Snapshots confirm only BirdyBeep entries are added and no secrets are emitted.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
This ticket IS the test deliverable. Run the snapshot suite (e.g. vitest snapshots) over the fixture set; verify it passes clean, then deliberately mutate the generator and confirm the suite fails (proving the guard works) before reverting. Snapshots run in CI and are hard-blocked by the pre-push hook — they are part of the §16.4 "tests for non-destructive config patching" requirement.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires "Claude Code — install user hooks" (snapshots capture its output and round-trip with uninstall).
- Directly implements the §16.4 public-repo requirement (tests for non-destructive config patching) and the §21.1 risk mitigation (snapshot tests for config generation so harness format changes are caught early).

## References
- PRD §16.4 (public agent repo must include tests for non-destructive config patching), §21.1 (snapshot tests for config generation as harness-change mitigation).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN. src/snapshot.test.ts (vitest file-based snapshots in src/__snapshots__/snapshot.test.ts.snap; __snapshots__ prettier-ignored so vitest owns its format). Committed snapshots: (1) from-scratch install — canonical BB hook block (6 events × {matcher:'', hooks:[{type:command, command:'birdybeep hook claude', timeout:10}]}); (2) patch into existing config with a same-event Stop hook + permissions (BB appended, user hook + permissions preserved); (3) patch into existing config with mcpServers + theme. Plus non-snapshot invariants: double-install idempotent (2nd output === 1st); install→uninstall byte-for-byte back to the original fixture; no token/Bearer/secret in any generated config.

GUARD PROVEN (§21.1, the ticket's deliberate-mutate test): clean run 5/5 → mutated the generator (HOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS 10→11) → 3 snapshots FAILED → reverted → 5/5 green again. So any drift in the generated config or Claude Code's hook format fails the suite. Full gate + pre-push green; 44 claude-code tests total.
close_reason: Config snapshot tests (§21.1): committed snapshots for from-scratch + patch-into-existing (2 fixtures), idempotency, install→uninstall round-trip, no-secrets; guard proven by mutate-then-fail-then-revert. Gate green.

title: birdybeep hook claude|codex|opencode
description:
`birdybeep hook claude|codex|opencode` — the local entrypoint that every installed adapter config invokes when its harness fires a lifecycle event. It reads the token, normalizes and redacts the event, sends with a short timeout, queues on failure, opportunistically drains the queue, and returns fast — the hot path that must never slow the coding harness.

## Scope
- Read the machine token from the secure token store; select the named harness's `normalizeEvent` to turn the raw harness payload (passed on stdin/args/env per that harness's hook contract) into a canonical BirdyBeep event.
- Redact/truncate the payload and hash absolute paths before any network call; enforce max payload size.
- Send via the Sender with a short timeout; on failure write to the local queue; ALSO opportunistically drain the local queue on each invocation (§9.3); then return quickly.
- Must never block or noticeably slow the harness — fast return is a hard requirement, online or offline.
- Non-goals: deciding whether to notify (that's the backend), durable/guaranteed delivery (queue is best-effort, 24h), interactive output.

## Acceptance criteria
- `birdybeep hook <harness>` accepts that harness's real event payload, normalizes it via the harness's `normalizeEvent`, and sends a canonical event to the backend within a short timeout.
- On send failure (offline/timeout) the event is written to the local queue and the command still returns fast and exits in a way that does not error the harness.
- Each invocation opportunistically drains queued events when the backend is reachable.
- Redaction/truncation and absolute-path hashing are applied before send; payload never exceeds the max size.
- Token is read from the token store, never from a config/repo file.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
- CLI/adapter verification in a temp HOME against the E2E harness: feed real captured Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode hook payloads into `birdybeep hook <harness>` and assert (1) the stub API receives the correctly-normalized canonical event for each harness, (2) with the stub unreachable the event lands in the temp-HOME queue and the command returns fast, (3) a subsequent invocation drains the queue and delivers, (4) the sent payload is redacted/truncated with absolute paths hashed, and (5) measured invocation latency stays within the fast-return budget so the harness isn't slowed. This is the core of `CLI-E2E` and `CLI-OFFLINE-QUEUE-E2E`.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires `Sender (short timeout, queue-on-fail)`, `Normalizer + redaction/truncation`, and the three per-harness mappings: `Claude Code — normalizeEvent mapping`, `Codex — normalizeEvent mapping`, `OpenCode — normalizeEvent mapping`.
- Privacy/reliability invariants are load-bearing: redact/truncate + hash paths before send, never persist bodies, token only from the token store, best-effort 24h queue, and fast non-blocking return (§9.2/§9.3).

## References
- PRD §9.2 (local command hook pattern), §9.3 (no daemon, queue, fast return), §9.4 (commands), §9.5-9.7 (per-harness event mappings). CLI is terminal-only — no Perch screen.

title: EPIC: @birdybeep/cli
description:
The `@birdybeep/cli` package — the open-source (MIT) command surface developers install with `npm install -g @birdybeep/cli` to pair a machine, install agent integrations, run hooks, and self-diagnose. This epic delivers every §9.4 command and is the human-facing entry point to the whole BirdyBeep agentic experience.

## Scope
- All §9.4 commands: `login`, `logout`, `status`, `test`, `doctor`, `agent install all|claude|codex|opencode`, `agent uninstall all|claude|codex|opencode`, `hook claude|codex|opencode`, plus the `queue clear` debug helper.
- A shared command framework with config dir, `--json`/`--non-interactive` flags, version and help; everything reads the machine token via the secure token store and never from repo files.
- The `hook` entrypoint that adapter configs invoke (normalize → redact → send → queue-on-fail → opportunistic drain → return fast), and the two mandatory real-install E2Es (login→install→hook→delivered, and offline-queue drain).
- Non-goals: project-level installs (user-level/global is the only MVP path), background daemon, and any harness adapter logic itself (lives in the agent-core and per-harness packages this CLI orchestrates).

## Success criteria
- A developer can go from `npm install -g @birdybeep/cli` → `birdybeep login` (QR or manual code) → `birdybeep agent install all` → real agent lifecycle event delivered to the backend and a push job enqueued, with no per-session QR scan.
- Installs are idempotent, back up existing harness config, add only BirdyBeep-managed entries, and uninstall removes only those entries; tokens are never written into repo-local files.
- Hooks return fast and never block or noticeably slow the coding harness, even offline; queued events drain opportunistically and arrive once connectivity returns.
- `birdybeep doctor` identifies common problems (token/integration/queue/network, `needs_trust`/`needs_restart`) with actionable fixes.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
- Epic test bar: every command is verified in a temp-HOME sandbox via the E2E test harness; no command is considered done on "it should work". The two epic-level gates are `CLI-E2E` (real login→install→hook→delivered against wrangler dev/staging, asserting the event reached `POST /v1/agent-events` and a push job was enqueued) and `CLI-OFFLINE-QUEUE-E2E` (backend unreachable → hook returns fast and queues → later hook/status/doctor drains and delivers, asserting the harness is never blocked). Both run in CI and are hard-blocked by the pre-push hook.

## Dependencies & notes
- Orchestrates the agent-core primitives: Machine token storage (keychain + file fallback), Local event queue (24h, strict perms), Sender (short timeout, queue-on-fail), Normalizer + redaction/truncation, and the AgentAdapter interface, plus the per-harness adapters.
- Privacy invariants run here: tokens shown once and stored hash-free only locally in keychain/strict-perm file, never in repo files; payloads redacted/truncated and absolute paths hashed before send.
- Cross-repo note: the receiving backend endpoints (`POST /v1/agent-events`, `POST /v1/integrations/status`, `POST /v1/test-beep`, the `/v1/pair/*` flow) live in the private product repo; these are coordination NOTES, not hard build links.

## References
- PRD §9.4 (CLI commands), §7.2 (pairing flow), §7.3 (agent installation flow), §9.1-9.3 (adapter strategy, hook pattern, no-daemon), §19.2 (CLI acceptance), §15.1/§15.3 (token + local-queue privacy). CLI is terminal-only — no Perch screen.

title: pnpm workspaces + build (tsup)
description:
Scaffold the public agent monorepo's workspace, shared TS/lint/format config, and build pipeline so every package compiles and ships from one `pnpm build`. This is the structural foundation the CLI, agent-core, and three adapters all live in.

## Scope
- pnpm workspaces with the exact PRD §16.3 layout: `packages/{cli,agent-core,claude-code,codex,opencode}`, plus `examples/{claude-code,codex,opencode}`, `docs/` (install, pairing, security, troubleshooting, adapter-development), and `scripts/` (release.ts, smoke-test.ts) — directories scaffolded even where content lands in later tickets.
- `cli` package is published as `@birdybeep/cli` (matches `npm install -g @birdybeep/cli`); adapters and agent-core scoped under `@birdybeep/*`.
- Shared `tsconfig` base (strict), eslint, and prettier at the root, consumed by every workspace.
- tsup build per package emitting ESM/CJS + type declarations; the `cli` package builds an executable `bin` entry.
- `LICENSE` (MIT) present at repo root; root `README` stub.
- Non-goal: implementing any adapter logic, CLI commands, or the event schema — only the buildable skeleton.

## Acceptance criteria
- A fresh clone + `pnpm install && pnpm build` succeeds with zero type or build errors across all five packages.
- `pnpm lint` and `pnpm typecheck` pass repo-wide from the shared config.
- Each package emits dist artifacts + `.d.ts` files; the `cli` package produces a runnable bin.
- `LICENSE` at root is MIT and `package.json` files declare `"license": "MIT"`.
- Workspace cross-imports (e.g. an adapter importing `agent-core`) resolve via workspace protocol.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
- Pure infra/chore proof: run `pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck` from a clean checkout and confirm all succeed; confirm dist + declaration outputs exist for every package and the CLI bin is executable (`node packages/cli/dist/index.js --help` or equivalent resolves). Add a trivial placeholder unit test per package and confirm `pnpm test` discovers and runs it, so CI and the pre-push gate have something real to execute. Verify a workspace cross-import builds (import a symbol from `agent-core` into one adapter package and confirm it type-checks). No screenshots — this is non-UI infra.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires the epic root `EPIC: Agent repo scaffolding (public, MIT)` (EPIC-A-FOUNDATION).
- Honor the PRD §16.3 directory names exactly so docs, examples, and release scripts have their expected homes. MIT licensing is a hard requirement: this repo is public precisely so developers can audit code that runs in their environments.

## References
- PRD §16.3 (public repo `birdybeep-agent` package/examples/docs/scripts layout), §7.2 (`npm install -g @birdybeep/cli`), §5.1 item 14 (integrations + CLI are open source under MIT).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN (agentic-first, all run locally on Node 22 / pnpm 10.28.2).

SCAFFOLD: pnpm workspace (packages/*) + turbo; PRD §16.3 layout exact — packages/{cli,agent-core,claude-code,codex,opencode} + examples/{claude-code,codex,opencode} + docs/{install,pairing,security,troubleshooting,adapter-development}.md + scripts/{release,smoke-test}.ts. Shared config at REPO ROOT (tsconfig.base.json strict, eslint.base.js factory, prettier.config.js) mirroring product ../birdybeep/packages/config — NOT a config package (kept §16.3 names exact). Build = tsup ESM+CJS+dts per package (mandated divergence from product's tsc). @birdybeep/cli publishable; 4 libs scoped @birdybeep/* (private for now — see publish-landmine memory). cli split into library entry (index.ts, real .d.ts) + bin entry (bin.ts, shebang) so the published d.ts isn't a bare shebang.

EVIDENCE (all EXIT 0): pnpm build/lint/typecheck/test/format:check green; SIMULATED FRESH CLONE (git checkout-index -> temp, pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, full pipeline) green; every pkg emits dist/index.{js,cjs,d.ts,d.cts}; cli bin (node dist/bin.js --help->0, --version->0.0.0, unknown->1, shebang+exec bit); workspace cross-import claude-code->agent-core (workspace:*) builds+typechecks+tested; lockfile carries full esbuild+rollup platform matrix (win32/linux) so CI frozen install resolves cross-OS.

REVIEW: ran 5-agent adversarial review; fixed 3 real findings (broken cli .d.ts -> split entries; engines node>=20.9 but import.meta.dirname needs >=20.11 -> bumped to >=20.11.0; added .gitattributes LF for Windows CI/snapshots). Deferred per-package READMEs (a-docs) and private->public publish flip (a-cli/a-release; see bd memory).
close_reason: pnpm/turbo/tsup monorepo scaffold complete; all 5 packages build (ESM+CJS+dts), lint/typecheck/test/format green, fresh-clone + frozen-lockfile proven, CLI bin runnable, workspace cross-import verified.

title: Normalizer + redaction/truncation
description:
Provides `normalizeEvent` and the redaction/truncation helpers that turn raw harness input into a privacy-safe, size-bounded `BirdyBeepAgentEvent` before anything is sent. This is where the local privacy rules run (PRD §9.2): hash absolute paths, strip/redact sensitive payload content, and enforce the max payload size so the harness never leaks code, prompts, or secret-bearing strings to the network.

## Scope
- `normalizeEvent(input: unknown): BirdyBeepAgentEvent` — coerce raw adapter input into the schema, fill defaults (e.g. generate `event_id`, set `occurred_at`), and return a validated event.
- Path hashing: replace absolute paths (`workspace.cwd`, any path-like values in `metadata`) with stable hashes; keep safe labels like `repo_name`/`branch` where present (§15, §14.5 privacy note).
- Redaction/truncation: truncate `title`, `body`, and `metadata` string values to bounded lengths; redact obviously sensitive tokens/secrets patterns; drop unknown oversized fields.
- Max-size enforcement: compute serialized payload size and enforce a hard cap before handing off to the sender; reject or shrink over-cap payloads deterministically.
- Non-goal: deciding whether to notify (server-side, §10.5), network delivery (CORE-SENDER), and on-disk storage (CORE-QUEUE).

## Acceptance criteria
- Output of `normalizeEvent` always passes `BirdyBeepAgentEvent` validation or throws a typed error — never returns a partially-valid event.
- No absolute filesystem path appears in the normalized output; path-like inputs are hashed and the hash is stable across runs for the same input.
- `title`/`body`/`metadata` strings are truncated to the documented caps; payloads exceeding the max size are shrunk or rejected, never sent over-cap.
- Redaction runs before any value leaves the function; the function performs no I/O and no logging of raw content.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
vitest property/unit tests: feed real raw payloads captured from Claude Code (`PermissionRequest`, `Stop`), Codex (`notify`), and OpenCode (`session.idle`, `permission.asked`) and assert the normalized output (a) validates, (b) contains zero absolute paths (regex/property assertion over the serialized output), (c) is under the size cap, and (d) has truncated long strings. Adversarial cases: inject secret-like strings and long paths into `metadata` and assert they are hashed/redacted/truncated, never echoed. A no-leak invariant test runs the normalizer over fuzzed input and fails if any path or over-cap string survives. Correctness is the green no-leak suite, not inspection.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires `Event schema + types (mirror product schemas)` (CORE-SCHEMA) for the target shape and validator.
- Privacy invariants this ticket enforces: hash absolute paths by default, never log raw title/body/metadata, bound payload size before send. These back the product-wide guarantee that notification title/body content is never persisted server-side and that the agent only sends metadata-safe events.

## References
- PRD §9.2 (redact/truncate in the local hook pattern), §10.2 (payload to produce), §15 (security/privacy: hash/redact paths, no body logging), §14.5 (hash absolute paths privacy note).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN. src/normalize.ts: normalizeEvent(input: unknown, opts?) -> validated BirdyBeepAgentEvent (or throws typed NormalizeError). Pure (crypto only; no I/O, no logging of raw content §15.2). Pipeline per string: scrubAbsolutePaths (POSIX ≥2-seg + Windows drive → stable h_<sha256[:16]>) → redactSecrets (AWS/GitHub/OpenAI/Slack/JWT/key=value) → truncate. cwd ALWAYS hashed (§15 no absolute path leaves machine); repo_name/branch kept as safe labels (§14.5). Fills event_id (evt_local_<uuid>) + occurred_at defaults (injectable for tests). metadata = open record, recursively sanitized, bounded (keys/depth/value length). enforceSize: deterministic shrink (drop metadata → harden body → harden title) under MAX_AGENT_EVENT_BYTES (16KB §13.5) or throw. Caps: title 200/body 2000/meta-val 500/label 120.

PROOF (src/normalize.test.ts, 13 tests): defaults+validity, injected clock/id determinism, preserve caller id/ts, throws NormalizeError on un-fixable (bad event_type), no-mutation; path hashing (cwd h_ format, stable, scrub embedded paths in title/body/source_session_id/metadata); secret redaction (5 shapes absent from output); truncation to caps; size cap (4000-key metadata forced under 16KB, still valid); nested metadata scrubbed; NO-LEAK INVARIANT over 200 adversarial drafts → zero absolute paths, under cap, valid every time. Full gate + pre-push green.

Note: §10.2 example shows raw cwd (illustrating the string field); §11 + this ticket mandate the agent hashes absolute paths before send — cwd:string accepts the hash, so no schema conflict / no lockstep divergence.
close_reason: normalizeEvent + redaction/path-hashing/truncation/16KB-cap; proven by 13 tests incl. a 200-iteration no-leak invariant (zero absolute paths/secrets, under cap, always valid). All gates green.

title: birdybeep agent install all|<harness>
description:
`birdybeep agent install all|<harness>` — detects supported coding harnesses on this machine and installs BirdyBeep's user-level integration config via each adapter, idempotently and non-destructively. This is the second half of the once-per-machine setup that makes sessions appear automatically.

## Scope
- Detect installed supported harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode) and run the requested adapter install(s): `all` installs every detected harness; `<harness>` targets one.
- Idempotent installs that add ONLY BirdyBeep-managed entries to user-level/global harness config, preserving existing config and backing it up before modification.
- Print the changed files and any required user action (e.g. "Codex may require one-time hook trust — open Codex and run `/hooks`", "Restart OpenCode for the plugin to load"), and run integration status checks after install (per §7.3 steps 5-7).
- Adapter configs must call `birdybeep hook <harness>`; durable tokens are never written into harness/repo config — the hook reads the token from the token store at runtime.
- Non-goals: project-level installs (user-level/global is the only MVP path), reporting status to the backend (that's `CLI-REPORT-STATUS`), the adapter-specific normalize/status logic (lives in per-harness packages behind the adapter interface).

## Acceptance criteria
- `agent install all` installs every detected harness; `agent install <harness>` installs exactly that one and reports cleanly when the harness isn't detected.
- Re-running install is idempotent — no duplicate entries, config unchanged on the second run.
- Existing harness config is backed up before modification, and only BirdyBeep-managed entries are added.
- The command prints the exact changed files and any required user action (Codex trust, OpenCode restart).
- No durable token is written into any harness config or repo-local file.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
- CLI/adapter verification in a temp HOME against the E2E harness: seed the temp HOME with fixture harness configs (including pre-existing user content), run `agent install all`, and assert (1) a backup of each modified config exists, (2) only BirdyBeep-managed entries were added and pre-existing content is intact, (3) the configs invoke `birdybeep hook <harness>` and contain NO token, (4) changed-files and required-action output is correct, and (5) a second run is a no-op (byte-identical config). Snapshot-test generated config per §21.1. The full install leg is also covered by `CLI-E2E`.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires `CLI framework + global structure` and the `AgentAdapter interface` (the detect/install/uninstall/status/doctor/normalizeEvent contract each harness implements).
- Idempotency/backup/privacy invariants are load-bearing here: idempotent, backs up before modifying, adds only managed entries, never writes tokens into repo/harness files.

## References
- PRD §7.3 (agent installation flow + acceptance: idempotent, backup, managed-only entries, no repo tokens, user-level default), §9.1 (adapter interface), §9.4, §9.5-9.7 (per-harness install behavior), §21.1. CLI is terminal-only — no Perch screen.

title: E2E test harness (temp HOME + stub API)
description:
Build the sandbox E2E harness that installs adapters into an isolated temp HOME, fires real harness hook payloads, and asserts the events actually arrive at a real BirdyBeep API. This is the backbone of every mandatory adapter E2E: it replaces "it should work" with a real install firing a real event verified end-to-end.

## Scope
- Sandbox runner that creates an isolated temp HOME (and isolated config dirs) per test so installs never touch the real machine, and tears it down afterward.
- A fake/local BirdyBeep API stub the harness can run, OR a `wrangler dev` instance of the real API, that records inbound `POST /v1/agent-events` for assertion; harness exposes which mode is in use.
- Helpers to: run a real adapter `install` into the temp HOME (Claude Code hooks / Codex notify+hooks / OpenCode plugin), assert the expected config files were written (and only BirdyBeep-managed entries added, existing config backed up), fire a real harness hook payload through `birdybeep hook <harness>`, and assert the normalized event was delivered to the stub.
- Provides a fast single-adapter smoke path (consumed by the pre-push hook) and a full path (consumed by CI).
- Privacy assertion helpers: confirm payloads are redacted/hashed where required and that no durable token is written into repo-local files.
- Non-goal: implementing the adapters themselves — the harness ships with at least one wired example proving the rig works end-to-end; remaining adapters plug in as they land.

## Acceptance criteria
- Running the harness for at least one adapter performs a real install into a temp HOME, fires a real hook payload, and asserts the matching event reached the stub API — with no writes outside the sandbox.
- After install, the harness asserts existing harness config is preserved + backed up and only BirdyBeep-managed entries were added (non-destructive, idempotent: a second install is a no-op).
- The harness asserts no token is written into any repo-local file in the sandbox.
- The smoke subset runs fast enough to gate pre-push; the full run is invocable in CI across the OS matrix.
- Sandbox is fully torn down (temp HOME removed) on success and failure.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
- This ticket IS the test infrastructure, so its proof is self-demonstrating: stand up the stub API (or `wrangler dev`), run the harness against one real adapter, and show (a) the adapter's config files appear in the temp HOME with only BirdyBeep-managed entries and a backup of pre-existing config, (b) firing the real harness payload via `birdybeep hook <harness>` results in the stub recording the expected normalized agent-event, and (c) a re-run of install is idempotent. Confirm the temp HOME is deleted afterward and that nothing was written under the real user HOME. Run on the current OS here; CI extends it across macOS/Ubuntu/Windows.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires `pnpm workspaces + build (tsup)` (A-MONOREPO) so adapter and CLI packages exist to install and invoke.
- Honor the install invariants from PRD §7.3: installation idempotent, existing config backed up before modification, only BirdyBeep-managed entries added, tokens never written into repo-local files — the harness must assert these, not assume them.
- Cross-repo note: when run in `wrangler dev` mode this exercises the real `POST /v1/agent-events` ingestion endpoint owned by the private product repo; the agent event schema must stay in lockstep with the product `packages/schemas` (a NOTE, not a hard link).

## References
- PRD §16.4 (public agent repo requirements: tests for non-destructive config patching), §19.2 (CLI & integrations acceptance: idempotent installer, backs up config, only BirdyBeep-managed entries, tokens not in repo files), §7.3 (agent installation flow + acceptance), §7.4 / §10.2 (session sync flow + canonical event payload the harness fires).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN. Built packages/test-harness (private @birdybeep/test-harness, never published — internal test infra, so it doesn't touch the §16.3 published surface). The spine every adapter E2E hangs off.

DESIGN (the 4 design musts):
- HERMETIC temp HOME (src/sandbox.ts): redirects HOME, USERPROFILE, HOMEPATH, XDG_{CONFIG,DATA,STATE,CACHE}_HOME, APPDATA, LOCALAPPDATA, AND TMPDIR/TMP/TEMP into a throwaway dir (so even os.tmpdir()/cache writes are contained); realpath's the real home; captures+restores original env (incl. was-unset → delete); rm -rf on success AND failure; sandbox.path() rejects ../absolute traversal. Cross-OS.
- SWAPPABLE sink (src/sink.ts): EventSink interface + working StubEventSink (in-process http capturing POST /v1/agent-events). Stub NOT hard-wired — helpers depend only on the interface + the adapter fires at a plain endpoint URL, so the product can inject a wrangler-dev EVT-INGEST sink for the eventual cross-repo E2E. No fake remote shipped.
- REAL-SHAPED fixtures (src/fixtures.ts): Claude Code hook payloads matching the official CC stdin schema (Notification{notification_type,message}, Stop{stop_reason}, SessionStart{source,model}); embed absolute cwd/transcript (must hash) + long message (must truncate).
- CONTRACT assertions (src/contract.ts): event_type/harness/session match + EXACT delivered count; size cap (16KB); assertPathsHashed (body+headers) + assertNoAbsolutePaths (catch-all for un-listed path leaks); assertTruncated; token via file-fallback store as Bearer but never echoed in body; captureTree/assertTreeDelta/assertTreesEqual (non-destructive + idempotent + byte-for-byte uninstall); assertNoTokenInRepo; assertRealHomeUntouched (realpath, uniquely-ours artifacts).

PROOF (self-demo src/harness.e2e.test.ts, green; reference adapter stands in for the real CC adapter until CC-E2E): install over FOREIGN ~/.claude/settings.json → backup is byte-for-byte original, only managed entries added (assertTreeDelta) → 2nd install no-op (idempotent) → fire real Notification payload → exactly 1 event delivered, event_type=approval_required, paths hashed (no raw cwd/transcript/home), no absolute path anywhere, body truncated, Bearer token from file store (not in body, not in repo), real HOME untouched → uninstall restores byte-for-byte → sandbox deleted. All 7 packages: pnpm lint/typecheck/test/build/format:check green.

ADVERSARIAL REVIEW: 4-agent review hunting for 'hollow ✅'. Fixed the real ones (TMPDIR/TMP/TEMP isolation, realpath home, traversal guard, header leak scan, assertNoAbsolutePaths catch-all, exact-count, endpoint-not-sink fidelity, longer hash). Rejected adversarial-sabotage findings (private-field reflection, hardcoded-path escape — not the threat model: we author the adapters) and the misread 'async race' (handler pushes BEFORE res.end). Deferred (bd remember): full CC fixture set + PermissionRequest reconciliation → a-claude; real redaction/hash policy + token format → agent-core CORE-NORMALIZER.

title: Machine token storage (keychain + file fallback)
description:
Implements secure storage and retrieval of the per-machine installation token: OS keychain when available, strict-permission file fallback otherwise, and never written into repo-local files. This is the single secure home for the credential the sender uses (PRD §7.2, §15.1).

## Scope
- `setToken`/`getToken`/`clearToken` for the machine installation token, namespaced to BirdyBeep.
- Prefer the OS keychain (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, Linux Secret Service / libsecret) where available.
- Strict-permission file fallback under the user data dir (file `0600`, dir `0700`) when no keychain is usable — e.g. SSH/headless Linux without a secret service.
- Never write the token into repo-local files or harness config; harness adapters reference the local command, not the token (§9.2).
- Token is shown once at pairing time and stored only as needed locally; server holds only the hash (§15.1). Support clearing on `logout`/revoke and overwrite on rotation.
- Non-goal: pairing/QR flow and token issuance (separate pairing tickets); server-side hashing/revocation.

## Acceptance criteria
- On a machine with a keychain, the token is stored in and read back from the keychain; the fallback file is not created.
- On a machine without a usable keychain, the file fallback is created with `0600` perms in the user data dir; a non-strict file is rejected/repaired.
- The token never appears in any repo-local file, harness config, or log output.
- `clearToken` removes the credential from both keychain and fallback; rotation overwrites cleanly.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
vitest tests against a temp `HOME`/isolated keychain namespace: round-trip set/get/clear via the keychain path; force the fallback path (simulate no secret service) and assert the file is created `0600` under the data dir and round-trips. Negative: pre-create a world-readable fallback file and assert it is rejected/repaired. Leak assertion: after storing a token, grep the temp `HOME` repo tree and generated harness config for the token string and assert zero hits, and assert no log line contains it. Cross-platform note: exercise the keychain adapter on the CI OS and the file fallback explicitly. Proof is the passing store/fallback/no-leak suite.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires the epic root `EPIC: agent-core (schema, queue, sender, tokens)` and `A-MONOREPO` (pnpm workspaces + build via tsup).
- Consumed by `Sender (short timeout, queue-on-fail)` (CORE-SENDER), which reads the token at send time.
- Invariants: keychain-first, strict-perm file fallback, token shown once, hash-only server-side, never written to repo files — directly supports the SSH/headless pairing requirement (manual code entry).

## References
- PRD §7.2 (CLI stores credentials in OS keychain when possible, strict-perm file fallback; works in SSH/headless), §15.1 (token handling: shown once, hash-only server-side, file fallback strict perms, never in repo files, rotation/revocation).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN. src/token-store.ts per §7.2/§15.1/SPEC§11: keychain-first with strict-perm FILE fallback, NEVER repo-local. TokenStore interface (get/set/clear, kind). FileTokenStore: file 0600 in dir 0700 under birdyBeepDataDir() (atomic write tmp+rename; repairs a too-permissive file on read; POSIX perms, no-op on Windows ACL). KeychainTokenStore wraps an injectable KeychainBackend; ships macosKeychainBackend() (real 'security' add/find/delete-generic-password, -U update; namespaced service=birdybeep account=machine-token) + unavailableKeychainBackend (Linux-no-secret-service / Windows → file fallback today; Secret Service/Cred Manager backends can be added later). High-level setToken/getToken/clearToken: set→primary (keychain if available else file, returns kind); get→keychain-first then file; clear→BOTH. FileTokenStore methods return Promise.resolve (sync internals) to satisfy the async interface without require-await.

PROOF (src/token-store.test.ts, 6 tests, hermetic — REAL OS keychain NEVER touched, keychain path tested via in-memory fake backend): keychain round-trip set/get/clear with NO fallback file created; file fallback creates 0600 under data dir + round-trips; world-readable file repaired to 0600 on read; clearToken empties BOTH keychain + file; rotation overwrites cleanly; NO-LEAK — token stored in sandbox data file (under sandbox home, not in repo) and assertNoTokenInRepo confirms zero hits in the repo tree (token runtime-generated, never a source literal). No logging of the token. Full gate + pre-push green.

NOTE: real macOS 'security' backend is shipped but not unit-tested against the live keychain (non-hermetic; would prompt/pollute). CI Linux/Windows exercise the file fallback (the path they actually use). Real-keychain round-trip is a future manual/E2E check.
close_reason: Machine token storage: keychain-first (injectable backend + real macOS security impl) with strict-perm 0600 file fallback under data dir, never repo-local; clear-both, rotation, world-readable repair. 6 hermetic tests incl. no-leak-in-repo. All gates green.

title: Local event queue (24h, strict perms)
description:
Implements the on-disk local retry queue used when network delivery fails, so a failed or offline Beep never blocks the coding harness. Per PRD §9.3 there is no background daemon: the queue holds events for 24h with strict file permissions and drains opportunistically on the next hook invocation or relevant CLI command.

## Scope
- On-disk queue under the BirdyBeep user data dir (not repo-local), created with strict permissions (dir `0700`, files `0600`).
- Enqueue a normalized `BirdyBeepAgentEvent`; dequeue/drain in FIFO-ish order; mark/remove on successful send.
- 24h retention: entries older than 24h are pruned on access; queue is best-effort, not a durable audit log (§15.3).
- Opportunistic drain hook the sender and CLI call (`birdybeep test`/`status`/`doctor`) trigger; drain must be bounded and non-blocking so it never noticeably slows the harness (§9.3).
- Provide a clear/reset path usable by `doctor`/debug tooling (e.g. queue clear).
- Non-goal: the actual HTTP POST and timeout logic (CORE-SENDER owns that); the queue exposes enqueue/drain primitives the sender drives.

## Acceptance criteria
- Queue files/dirs are created with strict perms; a world-readable queue dir is treated as an error/repaired.
- Entries older than 24h are not delivered and are pruned; fresh entries persist across process restarts.
- Enqueue and drain are bounded and never block the calling hook beyond a small budget; a full/locked queue degrades gracefully without throwing into the harness.
- No notification title/body is logged while queuing; queued payloads are the already-normalized, redacted events.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
vitest tests against a temp `HOME`: assert created dir/file modes are `0700`/`0600`; enqueue N events, restart-simulate, and confirm they survive; insert an event with a >24h timestamp and assert it is pruned, not drained. Integration: with `wrangler dev` serving `/v1/agent-events`, take the queue offline (point sender at a dead URL), fire a real harness payload so it lands in the queue, then bring the endpoint up and invoke a drain (`birdybeep test`/`doctor` against temp HOME) and assert the previously-queued event is delivered exactly once. Concurrency test: two simultaneous drains do not double-send. Proof is the passing perms + retention + drain-once suite.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires `Event schema + types (mirror product schemas)` (CORE-SCHEMA) for the stored event shape.
- Invariants: queue lives outside repo files, strict filesystem permissions, 24h best-effort retention, must not block harness execution.

## References
- PRD §9.3 (no daemon; short timeout then local queue; 24h retention; opportunistic drain; return quickly), §15.3 (local machine queue: 24h, best-effort, queue-clear via doctor).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN. src/queue.ts LocalEventQueue + src/paths.ts (cross-OS user data/config dirs honoring HOME/XDG/LOCALAPPDATA so it's never repo-local and stays sandbox-hermetic). No daemon (§9.3): enqueue parks a normalized event on disk (atomic write tmp+rename, file 0600, dir 0700); drain(send, {max}) is opportunistic + BOUNDED (default 50) and best-effort (every method swallows I/O errors — never throws into the harness). 24h retention (§15.3): entries older than QUEUE_RETENTION_MS pruned on access, never delivered; corrupt entries dropped. Perms repaired (chmod 0700) on every access. Drain-ONCE under concurrency: each entry claimed via atomic rename before send, so two concurrent drains partition work and never double-send. DrainOutcome delivered/drop → remove; retry → keep. clear() for doctor/debug. isSecure() perms check.

PROOF (src/queue.test.ts, 9 tests, hermetic via @birdybeep/test-harness createSandbox): default dir under user data dir + sandbox home, NOT in repo; 0700/0600 modes (POSIX); repairs a loosened dir on access; 24h prune (epoch entry pruned, not drained) + keep within window; survives restart (new instance same dir); delivered/drop/retry semantics; throwing sender → kept; bounded by max (100 enqueued, max 10 → 10 delivered, 90 left); clear() empties; CONCURRENCY — 2 simultaneous drains deliver each of 25 events exactly once (seen-count==1, total==25, queue empty). Perms tests guarded for Windows (ACL-based). Full gate + pre-push green.

DEFERRED: live wrangler-dev offline→drain E2E (cross-repo, needs EVT-INGEST) — covered later (dfl / product Phase 3); unit drain uses a stub send callback.
close_reason: On-disk retry queue: strict 0700/0600 perms (+repair), 24h prune, restart-survival, bounded best-effort drain, drain-once under concurrency, clear(). 9 tests in a hermetic temp HOME; all gates green.

title: EPIC: Agent repo scaffolding (public, MIT)
description:
Stand up the public, MIT-licensed `birdybeep-agent` monorepo: workspace layout, build, a cross-platform CI matrix, a hard pre-push gate, and the E2E harness that drives real adapter installs against a real API. This is the trust-and-correctness substrate every adapter and the CLI build on — the integration code runs inside developers' coding environments, so it must be auditable and verified end-to-end, never "it should work."

## Success criteria
- The repo at `birdybeep-agent` is public and MIT-licensed (LICENSE present at root), with the `packages/{cli,agent-core,claude-code,codex,opencode}` + `examples/` + `docs/` + `scripts/` layout from PRD §16.3 building cleanly from a fresh clone.
- A green CI run exists across macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows (CLI must work cross-platform): install, lint, typecheck, unit, and config-snapshot tests.
- A pre-push hook hard-blocks any push unless tests + config-snapshot + adapter smoke pass — the same mandatory-testing bar enforced in the product repo, mirrored here for the public repo.
- The E2E test harness runs adapters in an isolated temp HOME against a real BirdyBeep API stub (or `wrangler dev`), fires real harness payloads, and asserts delivered events — giving every phase-3 adapter ticket a real endpoint to verify against.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
- Epic-level bar: nothing in this epic is "done" on inspection alone. The monorepo proves a from-scratch `pnpm install && pnpm build` succeeds; CI proves the same on all three OSes; the pre-push hook proves it actually refuses a push when any gate is red (verified by an intentional failing run); the harness proves a real adapter install in a temp HOME emits a real event that the stub API receives. Every downstream adapter/CLI ticket reuses this harness for its own real-install, real-payload, delivered-event assertion.

## Dependencies & notes
- Root epic; no prerequisites. Children: `pnpm workspaces + build (tsup)` (A-MONOREPO), `CI matrix (macOS/Ubuntu/Windows)` (A-CI), `Pre-push hook (tests + snapshots + smoke)` (A-PREPUSH), `E2E test harness (temp HOME + stub API)` (A-TEST-HARNESS).
- Cross-repo note: this repo is public and MIT; product/backend code stays in the private `birdybeep` repo. The agent-core event schema (built in a later epic) must stay in lockstep with the product `packages/schemas` — but that lockstep is a NOTE, not a hard build link.
- Privacy/security invariants this scaffolding must make easy to honor downstream: tokens are never written into repo-local files, installers are idempotent and back up existing config, and adapters redact/hash paths before sending.

## References
- PRD §16.3 (public repo `birdybeep-agent` layout), §16.4 (public agent repo requirements: install/uninstall docs, security notes, `doctor`, non-destructive config-patching tests), §19.2 (CLI & integrations acceptance), §21.1 (snapshot tests for config generation).

title: EPIC: Claude Code adapter (highest priority)
description:
The Claude Code adapter — BirdyBeep's highest-priority integration (PRD §9.5) — delivered end to end: detect the install, patch user-level hooks non-destructively, map every Claude Code lifecycle event to a normalized BirdyBeep event, report health via status/doctor, cleanly uninstall, and prove it all with snapshot tests plus a real-hook E2E. This is the reference adapter the Codex and OpenCode adapters are modeled on.

## Success criteria
- A user who runs `birdybeep agent install claude` on a machine with Claude Code installed gets BirdyBeep-managed hooks added to `~/.claude/settings.json` (only BirdyBeep entries; existing config preserved and backed up; idempotent), and from then on Claude Code sessions surface in the app from their next lifecycle event.
- Every Claude Code hook event in the §9.5 mapping table (`SessionStart`, `PermissionRequest`, `Notification`, `Stop`, `StopFailure`, `SubagentStart`/`SubagentStop`, `TaskCreated`/`TaskCompleted`) produces the correct normalized `BirdyBeepEventType`, session-status transition, and default notify/no-notify decision.
- `status()` and `doctor()` accurately report `installed` / `needs_restart` / `error` and give actionable fixes; `uninstall()` removes only BirdyBeep entries and restores the pre-install backup, leaving `settings.json` byte-identical to the original where possible.
- The adapter implements the shared `AgentAdapter` interface unchanged and never writes a durable token into any config or repo file (the hook command reads the token from secure storage at runtime).
- All child tickets land green, including the mandatory real-hook E2E.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
The epic is not done until: (1) config snapshot tests cover the generated hook block and prove non-destructive patching against a hand-crafted pre-existing `settings.json`; and (2) the real-hook E2E (`CC-E2E`) installs the adapter into an isolated temp HOME, fires actual Claude Code hook payloads on stdin, and asserts the correctly-normalized events arrive at the stub API / wrangler-dev endpoint. "It should work" is never acceptable — a green snapshot suite plus a passing temp-HOME delivery assertion is the bar before any push, and the pre-push hook hard-blocks otherwise.

## Dependencies & notes
- Builds on the shared agent-core pieces in this repo: "AgentAdapter interface" (the contract every adapter implements), "Normalizer + redaction/truncation", and "Event schema + types (mirror product schemas)". The hook entrypoint each adapter's config calls is the CLI's `birdybeep hook claude|codex|opencode` command.
- Privacy invariants apply throughout: tokens are read from OS keychain / strict-perm file fallback at runtime and never written into config or repo files; absolute paths are hashed/redacted and the payload is truncated before send; notification title/body is never persisted by the adapter.
- Cross-repo note: the normalized events this adapter emits are validated server-side by the product repo's `/v1/agent-events` ingestion against `packages/schemas`; the agent-core schema must stay in lockstep with the product schemas.

## References
- PRD §9.5 (Claude Code integration + event mapping table), §9.1 (shared `AgentAdapter` interface), §10.1–10.2 (event types + canonical payload), §7.3 (install behavior / idempotency / backup), §19.2 (CLI & integration acceptance), §21.1 (harness-change mitigation via snapshot tests).
close_reason: EPIC complete: Claude Code adapter — detect/install/normalize/uninstall/status+doctor/snapshot + the real-hook E2E gate, all proven on the hermetic harness rig and shipped through the green pre-push gate + cross-OS CI. Codex/OpenCode lanes can now open.

title: CLI end-to-end (login→install→hook→delivered)
description:
The mandatory CLI end-to-end test: a real install proving the whole happy path — `login` (manual code) → `agent install all` → fire a real hook → event reaches the API and a push job is enqueued. This is the hard gate that makes "it should work" unacceptable for the CLI.

## Scope
- Drive the actual built CLI in a temp HOME against wrangler-dev (or staging): complete pairing via manual code, install all detected harnesses, and fire a real harness hook payload through `birdybeep hook <harness>`.
- Assert the event reached `POST /v1/agent-events` and that a push job was enqueued server-side as a result.
- Use the E2E test harness's helpers (temp HOME, adapter install, real hook firing, delivered-event assertions) — no mocks of the sender or normalizer; the real code paths run.
- Non-goals: unit-level coverage (lives with each command), mobile/web E2E.

## Acceptance criteria
- The test performs a real `login` (manual code), `agent install all`, and at least one real hook fire, all via the shipped CLI binary in an isolated temp HOME.
- It asserts the canonical event arrived at the ingestion endpoint and that a push job was enqueued.
- The test runs green in CI and is enforced by the pre-push hook; a regression in login, install, hook, sender, or normalize fails it.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
- This ticket IS the mandatory CLI E2E. Concrete proof: run it in CI and locally via the pre-push hook against `wrangler dev` (the product `EVT-INGEST` worker) in a temp HOME; assert (1) token minted + stored in temp HOME via manual-code login, (2) `agent install all` produced managed config invoking the hook, (3) a real hook payload produced a canonical event observed at `POST /v1/agent-events`, and (4) a push-dispatch job was enqueued. Failure of any leg blocks the push.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires `birdybeep hook claude|codex|opencode`, `birdybeep agent install all|<harness>`, and the `E2E test harness (temp HOME + stub API)`.
- Cross-repo note (xref): needs product `EVT-INGEST` (`POST /v1/agent-events`) running under wrangler dev — both are phase 3; coordination NOTE.
- Reinforces the agentic-first bar: every leg is verified against a real endpoint before push.

## References
- PRD §19.2 (CLI/integration acceptance: login with QR + manual code, `agent install all`, integrations send events). CLI is terminal-only — no Perch screen.

title: Claude Code — detect()
description:
Implements `detect()` for the Claude Code adapter: determine whether Claude Code is installed on this machine, capture its version, and resolve the user-level settings path the installer will patch. This is the gate every other Claude Code operation depends on.

## Scope
- Implement `detect(): Promise<DetectionResult>` per the shared `AgentAdapter` interface, returning at minimum: detected (boolean), `harness_version` when resolvable, and the resolved user settings path (default `~/.claude/settings.json`).
- Detection signals: presence of the Claude Code config dir (`~/.claude/`) and/or the `claude` binary on PATH; capture version via the binary where available, falling back to a not-resolved version rather than throwing.
- Resolve the settings path in a HOME-relative way (honors a non-standard `$HOME`) so the temp-HOME E2E and real installs both work; do not hard-code `/Users/...`.
- Return a clean `not_detected` result (no throw) when Claude Code is absent so `birdybeep agent install all` can skip it gracefully.
- Non-goals: no config mutation, no network calls, no event mapping (those are CC-INSTALL and CC-NORMALIZE).

## Acceptance criteria
- On a machine with Claude Code present, `detect()` returns detected=true with a settings path that exists or is the canonical default, and a version string when the binary reports one.
- On a machine without Claude Code, `detect()` returns detected=false and does not throw or create files.
- The resolved settings path is computed from the current `$HOME`, verified by pointing `$HOME` at a temp dir.
- Conforms exactly to the `AgentAdapter` `detect()` signature.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
Unit tests over a temp HOME: (1) seed a temp dir with `~/.claude/settings.json` and a stub `claude` on PATH → assert detected=true, correct resolved path, version parsed; (2) point `$HOME` at an empty temp dir with no `claude` binary → assert detected=false, no files created, no throw; (3) settings-path resolution follows `$HOME` (assert the returned path is under the temp HOME). These run under the standard adapter unit suite; CI/pre-push hard-block on green.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires "AgentAdapter interface" (the `detect/install/uninstall/status/doctor/normalizeEvent` contract) and the epic root "EPIC: Claude Code adapter (highest priority)".
- Detection must be side-effect-free and HOME-relative so the mandatory temp-HOME E2E and `agent install all` skip-on-absent behavior both work.

## References
- PRD §9.5 (Claude Code integration), §9.1 (`AgentAdapter` interface).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN. Built the real Claude Code adapter skeleton implementing agent-core's AgentAdapter: src/paths.ts (claudeConfigDir/claudeSettingsPath, HOME-relative — never hard-coded /Users), src/detect.ts (detectClaudeCode: side-effect-free, never throws; detected if ~/.claude exists OR best-effort 'claude --version' probe resolves; injectable home + probeVersion for tests; returns DetectionResult {detected, configPath=~/.claude/settings.json, version?}), src/adapter.ts (claudeCodeAdapter: AgentAdapter — detect() live; install/uninstall/status/doctor/normalizeEvent are rejecting stubs naming their tickets, replaced as the a-claude tickets land). Replaced the A-MONOREPO placeholder index.ts/index.test.ts.

PROOF (src/detect.test.ts, 5 tests, hermetic temp HOME via @birdybeep/test-harness): detects via seeded ~/.claude + parses probed version + configPath under sandbox HOME; detects via binary alone; not-detected returns no throw + creates NO files; settings path follows /Users/rebeccadespoiu; the adapter's no-arg detect() resolves under the sandbox HOME. Full gate + pre-push green.
close_reason: Claude Code detect(): HOME-relative, side-effect-free, never-throws; detected via ~/.claude or claude --version; resolves settings path. 5 hermetic temp-HOME tests. All gates green.

title: Sender (short timeout, queue-on-fail)
description:
Implements the sender that POSTs a normalized event to `POST /v1/agent-events` with a short timeout and, on any failure or timeout, writes the event to the local queue and returns fast — the reliability contract that keeps the coding harness responsive (PRD §9.2-9.3). It also opportunistically drains the queue on each send.

## Scope
- `send(event)` reads the machine token securely (via the token store), attaches auth, and POSTs the redacted event to the product `/v1/agent-events` endpoint.
- Short, hard timeout on the request; on timeout/network error/5xx, enqueue to the local queue and return without throwing into the harness.
- On each invocation, opportunistically drain pending queued events (bounded) before/after the live send.
- Fast return: total wall-clock budget kept small so the hook does not noticeably slow the harness (§9.3).
- Distinguish retryable failures (queue) from permanent rejects (e.g. revoked token / quota) so the queue does not fill with un-deliverable events.
- Non-goal: server-side ingestion, dedupe, or notification decisioning (product EVT-INGEST); queue storage internals (CORE-QUEUE); redaction (CORE-NORMALIZE).

## Acceptance criteria
- A successful POST within the timeout does not enqueue; the response is handled and the call returns quickly.
- A timeout or transport failure enqueues the event and returns without surfacing an error to the harness.
- The token is read from secure storage at send time and never logged; request bodies/title/body are not logged.
- Queue is drained opportunistically on send; permanent rejects are not re-queued indefinitely.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
vitest unit tests with a stubbed transport: assert timeout fires within budget and routes to enqueue; assert 2xx clears, 5xx/timeout queues, and a permanent reject (e.g. 401 revoked) does not re-queue. Integration against `wrangler dev`: fire a real Claude Code/Codex/OpenCode harness payload through `normalizeEvent` → `send` from a temp `HOME` and assert the delivered event lands at `/v1/agent-events` (delivered-event assertion). Offline path: point the sender at an unreachable URL, confirm the event is queued and the call still returns within the fast budget, then restore and confirm drain delivers it once. Latency assertion confirms the hook returns under the documented budget so the harness is not slowed.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires `Local event queue (24h, strict perms)` (CORE-QUEUE) for the queue-on-fail/drain path and `Normalizer + redaction/truncation` (CORE-NORMALIZE) for the payload it sends. Reads the token via the keychain/file token store.
- Cross-repo note (xref): the product `POST /v1/agent-events (ingestion)` (EVT-INGEST) is the receiving endpoint — match its auth header and payload contract; this is a note, not a hard link.
- Invariants: never block the harness, fast return, no token/body logging.

## References
- PRD §9.2 (send with short timeout, queue on failure, return quickly), §9.3 (no daemon; short timeout then queue), §13.4-13.5 (`/v1/agent-events` surface and ingestion requirements).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN. src/api.ts mirrors product packages/schemas/api.ts (ticket 95e, landed in product commit bcd93bb): ERROR_CODES ×9, errorCodeSchema/ErrorCode, errorEnvelopeSchema {error:{code,message,details?},requestId?}, ERROR_STATUS map, successEnvelope — LOCKSTEP. src/sender.ts createSender({baseUrl,timeoutMs,queue,tokenOptions,fetchImpl,drainMax}) → send(event)/drainNow(). send: reads token via token store at send time; POSTs redacted event to /v1/agent-events with Bearer auth + AbortController short timeout (default 3s); classifies the response via the error envelope CODE (rate_limited/internal_error → retry/queue; unauthorized/forbidden/token_revoked/validation_failed/payload_too_large/not_found/quota_exceeded → terminal/drop) with status fallback (5xx/429 retry else drop); enqueues on retry, drops terminal (no infinite re-queue), delivered on 2xx; then opportunistically drains the backlog (bounded by drainMax). No token → queue (retry after login), no network call. Never throws into the harness; never logs token/body.

PROOF (api.test.ts 4 parity + sender.test.ts 11, stubbed transport, hermetic sandbox queue + injected token backend): envelope parity (codes/shape/status match vendored product); 2xx delivers (no enqueue); Bearer Authorization carries the token; TIMEOUT (50ms) enqueues + returns <1s (fast budget); 5xx + 429 rate_limited queue; permanent rejects (unauthorized/token_revoked/quota_exceeded/validation_failed) drop + never re-queue; no-token queues without calling fetch; opportunistic drain flushes a 2-event backlog on a healthy send; console-spy confirms the token never appears in any log. Full gate + pre-push green.

DEFERRED: live wrangler-dev delivered-event + offline→restore→drain-once E2E (cross-repo, needs EVT-INGEST) — the unit suite proves the routing/timeout/classify logic; the real delivery is the held cross-repo gate.
close_reason: Sender: short-timeout POST to /v1/agent-events, queue-on-fail, error-envelope-driven retry-vs-terminal (mirrors product api 95e), opportunistic bounded drain, fast return, token-from-store never logged. 15 tests (envelope parity + reliability). All gates green.

title: Claude Code — normalizeEvent mapping
description:
Implements `normalizeEvent()` for Claude Code: pure mapping logic that turns a raw Claude Code hook payload (delivered on stdin, keyed by `hook_event_name`) into a canonical `BirdyBeepAgentEvent` with the right event type, session status, and notify default. This is the semantic core of the adapter and is intentionally decoupled from config install so it can be tested in isolation.

## Scope
- Implement `normalizeEvent(input: unknown): Promise<BirdyBeepAgentEvent>` per the `AgentAdapter` interface, mapping each Claude Code hook event to its BirdyBeep event per the §9.5 table:
  - `SessionStart` → `session_started` / `session_resumed` (status upsert; no notify)
  - `PermissionRequest` → `approval_required` (status `waiting_for_approval`; notify)
  - `Notification` → `needs_input` (status `waiting_for_input`; notify)
  - `Stop` → `agent_completed` (status `completed`; notify, user-disableable)
  - `StopFailure` → `agent_failed` (status `failed`; notify)
  - `SubagentStart` → `subagent_started` (running; no notify) / `SubagentStop` → `subagent_completed` (no notify for MVP)
  - `TaskCreated` → `task_created` / `TaskCompleted` → `task_completed` (running; no notify)
- Populate the canonical payload (§10.2): `event_type`, `occurred_at`, `harness:"claude_code"`, `harness_version`, `source_session_id` (from the hook's `session_id`), `machine`, `workspace` (cwd/repo_name/branch where derivable), `status`, and a human-readable `title`/`body` for the notification (e.g. "Claude Code needs approval").
- When Claude Code provides no stable session id, fall back to a best-effort id from available fields (cwd, transcript path, time window) per §10.3 — but delegate hashing/redaction/truncation of paths and the title/body to the shared normalizer/redaction layer rather than re-implementing it.
- Validate output against the shared Zod `BirdyBeepAgentEvent` schema; reject/clearly-error unknown or malformed hook payloads. Non-goal: no network send, no config I/O, no queueing (those are the sender/CLI hook path).

## Acceptance criteria
- Every event in the §9.5 table maps to the correct `BirdyBeepEventType`, `AgentSessionStatus`, and notify-default, verified by an explicit per-event assertion.
- The emitted payload validates against the agent-core event schema and carries `harness:"claude_code"` plus `source_session_id` derived from the hook's `session_id` (or a deterministic best-effort id when absent).
- Unknown/garbled hook payloads produce a typed error, not a malformed event.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
Table-driven unit tests using real Claude Code hook payload fixtures (one per §9.5 event, shaped like the documented stdin JSON: `session_id`, `cwd`, `hook_event_name`, `tool_name`, `tool_input`): assert event type, status, notify default, and schema validity for each. Add a fixture with a missing `session_id` → assert deterministic best-effort id. Add a malformed payload → assert typed rejection. Pure-logic suite, no HOME/network needed; CI/pre-push hard-block on green. The same fixtures are exercised live by CC-E2E.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires "Claude Code — detect()" (harness identity/version context), "Normalizer + redaction/truncation" (shared path-hashing, truncation, max-size enforcement — do not duplicate it here), and "AgentAdapter interface".
- Privacy invariants: absolute paths are hashed/redacted and the payload truncated to max size via the shared normalizer before any send; the adapter itself never persists title/body.
- Cross-repo note: output must satisfy the product repo's `packages/schemas` `BirdyBeepAgentEvent`; agent-core's schema mirror must stay in lockstep.

## References
- PRD §9.5 (Claude Code event mapping table), §10.1 (normalized event types), §10.2 (canonical payload), §10.3 (session identity / best-effort id), §10.5 (notify defaults).

Pure mapping → then agent-core normalizeEvent (CORE-NORMALIZE) does path-hash/redact/truncate/size-cap/validate (NOT re-implemented). Mapping (§9.5→§10.1, also fixed docs/SPEC.md §9.5 to reality): SessionStart→session_started/session_resumed (by source); Notification permission_prompt→approval_required, idle_prompt→agent_idle, other→needs_input; PermissionRequest→approval_required (dedupe w/ permission_prompt at CC-E2E); Stop→agent_completed; StopFailure→agent_failed (error_type into metadata); SubagentStop→subagent_completed. source_session_id from session_id or deterministic best-effort hash of cwd|transcript|event (§10.3). title: CLI framework + global structure
description:
The CLI framework and global structure every other command plugs into: command routing, config directory, global flags, version, and help. This is the foundation that makes the rest of the `birdybeep` command surface consistent and scriptable for both humans and agents.

## Scope
- A command framework (e.g. a small commander/yargs-style dispatcher) wiring up the §9.4 command tree with subcommands (`agent install <harness>`, `hook <harness>`, etc.).
- A config directory under the user's home (e.g. `~/.config/birdybeep` / OS-appropriate) for non-secret CLI config; the machine token itself is read via the secure token store, never duplicated here.
- Global flags: `--json` (machine-readable output for agents/scripts), `--non-interactive` (no prompts, fail fast when input would be required), `--version`, and `--help` at root and per-command.
- Consistent output helpers (human-readable + `--json` mode) and a shared exit-code convention so callers can branch on success/failure.
- Non-goals: any network/auth logic (login owns that), adapter logic, or persisting secrets in the config dir.

## Acceptance criteria
- `birdybeep --help` lists every §9.4 command; each subcommand has its own `--help`.
- `birdybeep --version` prints the package version; `--json` produces valid parseable JSON for commands that support structured output.
- `--non-interactive` never prompts and returns a non-zero exit code if a required value is missing instead of hanging.
- The config dir is created on first run with the right location per OS; the machine token is resolved through the token store, and no token value is ever written into the config dir.
- Unknown commands/flags produce a clear error and non-zero exit.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
- CLI/adapter verification in a temp HOME: run the built CLI binary with `HOME`/config dir pointed at a fresh temp directory and assert `--help`, `--version`, `--json`, and `--non-interactive` behaviors, including exit codes for unknown commands and for a required-but-missing value under `--non-interactive`. Assert the config dir is created in the temp HOME and contains no token material. Wire these as CLI unit/integration tests that the pre-push hook and CI run.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires `EPIC: @birdybeep/cli` (epic root) and `Machine token storage (keychain + file fallback)` so the shell can resolve the token without ever reading repo files.
- Privacy invariant: the config dir holds only non-secret config; the token lives exclusively in the keychain or a strict-perm fallback file.

## References
- PRD §9.4 (CLI command list). CLI is terminal-only — no Perch screen.

title: EPIC: agent-core (schema, queue, sender, tokens)
description:
Ships the shared agent-core library (`packages/agent-core` in the public MIT `birdybeep-agent` repo) that every harness adapter and the CLI build on: the canonical event schema, the normalizer/redactor, the 24h on-disk retry queue, the fast queue-on-fail sender, secure machine token storage, machine fingerprint/label/OS detection, and the `AgentAdapter` interface. This is the privacy-and-reliability backbone for session sync (PRD §9.1-9.3, §10): one schema, one auth/token layer, one queue, one sender, behind a single adapter contract.

## Success criteria
- All six core primitives exist, are exported from `agent-core`, and are consumed by at least one adapter and the CLI without duplication: schema+types, normalizer (redaction/truncation/path-hashing/size-enforcement), 24h strict-perm queue, short-timeout queue-on-fail sender, keychain+file-fallback token store, and machine fingerprint/label/OS detection.
- The `AgentAdapter` interface (`detect/install/uninstall/status/doctor/normalizeEvent`) is defined and implementable; `normalizeEvent` returns a validated `BirdyBeepAgentEvent`.
- An end-to-end agent path works with no per-session pairing: a real harness payload is normalized, redacted, and either POSTed to `/v1/agent-events` within the short timeout or written to the local queue, and the queue drains opportunistically on the next invocation — never blocking the harness.
- Privacy invariants hold across every primitive: no notification title/body is persisted or logged client-side, absolute paths are hashed, and durable tokens never land in repo files.
- The agent-core schema stays in lockstep with the product `packages/schemas` source of truth.

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
Epic test bar: every child ticket is hard-blocked by the pre-push hook + CI. Core library logic is covered by unit/property tests (Zod round-trips, redaction never leaks paths/secrets, queue retention/perms, sender timeout/queue-on-fail). The integration bar is a real install into a temp `HOME` (isolated keychain/config) that fires real Claude Code / Codex / OpenCode harness payloads through the core path against a live `wrangler dev` instance of the product API, and asserts the delivered event arrives at `/v1/agent-events` (or is queued and later drained). No "it should work": agents verify the delivered-event assertion and queue-drain behavior end-to-end before pushing.

## Dependencies & notes
- Foundation: requires `A-MONOREPO` (pnpm workspaces + build via tsup) so `agent-core` builds and is importable by sibling packages.
- Cross-repo note (xref): the product `packages/schemas — Zod schemas for events & API` (FOUND-SCHEMAS) is the source of truth; keep the agent schema in sync. The product `POST /v1/agent-events (ingestion)` (EVT-INGEST) is the receiving endpoint the sender targets. Cross-repo links are notes, not hard deps.
- Invariants enforced repo-wide: idempotent installs, back up before modifying config, hash-only tokens, never persist/log title/body, hash absolute paths.

## References
- PRD §9.1 (shared schema/auth/queue/sender + adapter interface), §9.2 (local command hook pattern, redact/truncate), §9.3 (no daemon, 24h queue, opportunistic drain), §10 (event model), §15 (token handling, content storage, local storage). Repo layout: §16.3 `birdybeep-agent/packages/agent-core`.
close_reason: EPIC complete: all 7 agent-core tickets closed (schema, normalize, queue, token-store, fingerprint, adapter-interface, sender), each proven with real vitest suites and shipped through the green pre-push gate + cross-OS CI. Cross-repo lockstep honored (event schema + 95e error envelope mirrored from product packages/schemas; live wrangler-dev delivery E2E held for product Phase 3).

title: Event schema + types (mirror product schemas)
description:
Defines the canonical `BirdyBeepAgentEvent` payload, the `BirdyBeepEventType` and `AgentSessionStatus` unions, and the `machine`/`workspace`/`metadata` sub-shapes as Zod schemas plus inferred TypeScript types — the typed contract every adapter, the normalizer, and the sender share. This mirrors the product `packages/schemas` so client and server validate the same shape.

## Scope
- `BirdyBeepEventType` union exactly per PRD §10.1: `session_started`, `session_resumed`, `session_active`, `needs_input`, `approval_required`, `agent_idle`, `agent_completed`, `agent_failed`, `test_failed`, `tool_started`, `tool_finished`, `subagent_started`, `subagent_completed`, `custom`.
- `AgentSessionStatus` union per §10.4: `starting`, `running`, `waiting_for_input`, `waiting_for_approval`, `idle`, `completed`, `failed`, `unknown`.
- `BirdyBeepAgentEvent` Zod schema matching the canonical payload (§10.2): `event_id`, `event_type`, `occurred_at` (ISO 8601), `harness` (`claude_code | codex | opencode`), `harness_version`, `source_session_id`, `machine {label, os}`, `workspace {cwd, repo_name, branch}` (optional fields), `status`, `title`, `body`, `metadata` (open record).
- Export both the Zod schemas and the inferred types; provide a `parse`/`safeParse` validation entry point used by adapters and the sender.
- Session-identity fields surfaced for downstream keying (§10.3): `harness` + `source_session_id` (+ machine/user supplied server-side).
- Non-goal: redaction, truncation, or path hashing (CORE-NORMALIZE owns that); no network or storage logic here.

## Acceptance criteria
- A payload matching the §10.2 example validates; missing required fields or out-of-union `event_type`/`status` values fail with clear Zod errors.
- Inferred TS types compile and are exported from `agent-core`; the event-type and status unions are exhaustive against §10.1/§10.4.
- Optional `workspace.*` and `metadata` are accepted when absent; `occurred_at` is validated as a datetime.
- Schema shape is field-for-field compatible with the product schemas package (same names, same enums).

## Testing (mandatory — agentic-first)
vitest unit + property tests: round-trip the §10.2 canonical payload through `safeParse` (valid), plus negative cases for each required-field omission and each bad-enum value; fuzz `metadata` with arbitrary records to confirm it is accepted without leaking. Cross-schema parity test: assert the agent `BirdyBeepEventType`/`AgentSessionStatus`/payload keys match the product `packages/schemas` definitions (snapshot or shared-fixture comparison) so drift fails CI. Proof of correctness is the passing schema suite — no "looks right" sign-off.

## Dependencies & notes
- Requires the epic root `EPIC: agent-core (schema, queue, sender, tokens)` and `A-MONOREPO` (pnpm workspaces + build via tsup) so the package builds and types export.
- Cross-repo note (xref): the product `packages/schemas — Zod schemas for events & API` (FOUND-SCHEMAS) is the source of truth — keep this in lockstep; any field/enum change there must be mirrored here.

## References
- PRD §10.1 (event types), §10.2 (canonical payload), §10.3 (session identity), §10.4 (session statuses).
notes:
DONE + PROVEN. Mirrored product packages/schemas field-for-field into agent-core: src/primitives.ts (BIRDYBEEP_EVENT_TYPES ×14 §10.1, AGENT_SESSION_STATUSES ×8 §10.4, HARNESS_IDS ×3, isoDateTimeSchema, MAX_AGENT_EVENT_BYTES + isWithinMaxAgentEventSize §13.5) + src/event.ts (machine/workspace/eventMetadata[catchall unknown]/birdyBeepAgentEventSchema §10.2 + inferred types + agentEventsRequestSchema alias). Enums vendored locally (agent repo can't import private @birdybeep/shared); values identical to product. Added zod ^4 dep.

PROOF (src/schema.test.ts, 10 tests, + index.test.ts 2): PARITY — vendored the EXACT product §10.2 canonical fixture (src/__fixtures__/canonical-event.json) and assert birdyBeepAgentEventSchema.safeParse(fixture).success===true; enum .options arrays equal the vendored product unions in order. VALIDATION — invalid events reject: missing event_type/machine, unknown event_type (error names event_type), out-of-union status, non-ISO occurred_at. OPTIONAL — minimal payload (no optional fields) valid; open metadata accepts arbitrary keys. SIZE — cap guard. Full gate green (lint/typecheck/test/build/format) + pre-push.

LOCKSTEP NOTE: vendored fixture + enum arrays must be updated whenever product packages/schemas / @birdybeep/shared changes (§16.4). Cross-repo drift detection beyond the vendored snapshot needs a shared fixture / CI cross-check (future).
